title: Wild Spicy Chinese Daikon Spring Rolls
description: A fiery dim‑sum style spring roll that pairs crisp daikon with deep‑fried agemono and a bold chili glaze, perfect for a quick, egg‑free snack.
instructions: Boil 1 cup of water in a pot until rolling, then add daikon, cabbage, mushrooms, bamboo shoots, and chili flakes, and simmer for 5 minutes to soften the vegetables.
Drain the vegetables and stir in soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, and maple syrup, then let the mixture cool to room temperature.
In a small bowl whisk together flour and water to make a smooth, slightly thick batter.
Heat a deep‑fryer or large pot of oil on a binchotan grill to 180°C (350°F).
Spoon about a tablespoon of the batter into a heat‑proof silicone cup and place it in the hot oil; fry until the batter is golden and crisp, about 1–2 minutes.
Remove the fried batter from the oil, drain on paper towels, and let it cool slightly while you prepare the filling.
Spread a thin layer of the cooled vegetable mixture onto each fried batter piece, leaving a small border around the edges.
Fold the batter in half over the filling, then fold the ends in to form a rectangular spring roll shape.
Brush each roll lightly with sesame oil and place them on a preheated binchotan grill, cooking for 2–3 minutes per side until the exterior is crisp and slightly charred.
Serve the rolls hot with a side of lime wedges for squeezing and a drizzle of extra soy sauce for dipping.
